value_chain_position: Emerging-market payment processing rails for global merchants — sells a cross-border payment infrastructure layer enabling multinationals and global e-commerce companies to accept local payments, disburse payouts, and navigate FX in LatAm, Africa, and South/Southeast Asia.
relative_comps: DLO is among the most load-bearing EM payment rail operators: its value is the regulatory/compliance/local-bank-connectivity moat that multinationals cannot replicate in-house across 40+ markets. 56% YoY and 38% 3-year CAGR with 33% FCF margin are best-in-class for its category. Direct competitors are largely private (Ebanx, Unlimint) or larger-platform incumbents (Adyen, Stripe) entering EM corridors. STNE/PAGS compete in Brazil specifically. At $3.5B mcap on $1.2B revenue with 33% FCF margin, DLO appears undervalued relative to its infrastructure position — the risk is regulatory/FX volatility in its operating markets.
